recent.statement: A tropical cyclone will make landfall in the Philippines within the next week
recent.evidence: The only cyclone signals (BERTHA, FAUSTO, ELIDA) are located in the Atlantic/Pacific far from the Philippines with zero population affected, and no signal shows any cyclone landfall in the Philippines.

recent.statement: WTI crude oil prices will rise above $95/bbl within 24 hours due to Red Sea shipping disruptions
recent.evidence: WTI crude is at $89.32/bbl (-3.11%), well below the $95 threshold, so the forecast did not come true.

recent.statement: A heatwave will continue to affect Germany within the next week
recent.evidence: No captured signal concerns this forecast, and a continuing heatwave in Germany would certainly have been captured before the window closed.

recent.statement: WTI crude oil price will rise above $95/bbl due to escalating Red Sea maritime disruptions
recent.evidence: WTI crude is at $89.32/bbl (down 3.11%), well below the $95 threshold, so the forecast did not come true.

recent.statement: The Strait of Hormuz will experience a temporary closure due to a naval conflict within the next week
recent.evidence: S4 shows the Strait of Hormuz still had 15 daily transits (only 30% below average), indicating it remained operational with no closure or naval conflict captured.

recent.statement: Red Sea maritime disruptions will push WTI crude above $95/bbl within 24 hours
recent.evidence: WTI crude is at $89.28/bbl (down 3.16%), well below the $95 threshold required by the forecast.

recent.statement: A major wildfire will break out in California's Napa Valley region.
recent.evidence: No admissible signal reports a major wildfire in Napa Valley during the window, so an event that large would have been captured yet none appears.

recent.statement: A Category 1 tropical cyclone will make landfall in the Philippines.
recent.evidence: The only cyclone signals (BERTHA, FAUSTO, ELIDA) are far from the Philippines with zero Category 1 wind impact, and no signal shows any Category 1 landfall in the Philippines during the closed window.

recent.statement: A devastating flood will hit the city of Paris within the next week
recent.evidence: No signal reports any flood in Paris, and a devastating flood would certainly have been captured within the closed window.

recent.statement: The price of oil will rise due to disruptions in the Bab el-Mandeb Strait
recent.evidence: Brent Crude fell to 96.39 $/bbl (-4.27%), showing oil prices declined rather than rose during the window.

recent.statement: Oil prices (WTI/Brent) will rise above $105/bbl due to intensified Red Sea attacks disrupting shipping
recent.evidence: Brent Crude is at $96.39/bbl (-4.27%), well below the $105 threshold and falling, so the forecast did not come true.

recent.statement: WTI crude oil prices will exceed $105 per barrel due to escalating Red Sea attacks disrupting shipping through the Strait of Hormuz
recent.evidence: Brent crude sits at $96.39 (down 4.27%), so WTI—typically below Brent—did not exceed $105, and no signal shows any such price spike.

self_calibration.note: Published headline probability is RAW conviction (unchanged); this is the honest companion. Calibration fixes overconfidence, not discrimination — see /calibration.
